programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Sixty-five-year-old Heather is rushed to Queens after shes found collapsed in the park. Deanna, long serving member of the team, speaks of her previous struggles with addiction.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien restore a metal work bench, a pine chest and the seat from an old pony and trap.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Niki and Rob have worked hard and saved for a deposit for their first home and are searching with a generous budget of £450,000. Meanwhile, Georgie and Louis love London, but dont live there.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Matt reveals a big makeover project for the farm, but before they can start building, theres demolition to be done. Luke takes on the task of fixing up the familys old Land Rover, and the farm celebrates a new addition.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Spain, rookie hoteliers Jo and Ken hope to boost their business, and mum and daughter Becky and Kay start a top-to-bottom renovation of a BandB.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Newly-weds Bev and Errol cant wait to buy themselves the ultimate wedding present - their very own holiday home in Mar Menor, Spain. Can Jasmine Harman find them the one for their £65k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: After hitting a brick wall on their search, how are Kate and Wayne getting on in Murcia? Building one apparently! And what has Keith done to his French fancy?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Nat and Dave are ready to buy a holiday home on Lanzarote, a place they visit twice a year. Jean tries to find their perfect property on the island they love so much.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cardiff, 41-year-old dog mad shop manager Nicola Steele is serving prawn cocktail and a meat-free lasagne. She also has some doggy surprises and unusual entertainment up her sleeve.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second to host in Cardiff is king of the name droppers Jerry Lockett, who hosts his night at his cousins mansion, following a flood at his home, serving dollops of food as well as a huge side order of celebrity.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: No-nonsense army wife Rachael Smith is third to host in Cardiff. Rachael is extremely competitive and isnt used to losing. She fully intends to win the prize and things might get ugly if she doesnt.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant costume designer Sam Clarke plans a Mexican menu on his night in Cardiff; and it turns out to be prophetic choice for an evening that is full of sizzle and spice. Two of the guests fight at the table.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Child minder Andrea Grainger plans a traditional Welsh menu and a warm welcome on her night in Cardiff. If this doesnt work she has some fun and games lined up to help her win the prize.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: We look back at the Clayton-Fisher family, who wanted to transform their dark and dingy attic. Struggling for space in their two-bed terrace, Sarah thought it was time to move on, but Pepper dug her heels in and wanted to convert the loft.

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: A couple create an ambitious floating home, two siblings build their own mobile homes away from home, and George and Will conjure up a magical 30-mile vista in a wall just 20cm thick, in their secret-garden studio flat.

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny follows brave buyers transforming properties bought at auction from derelict to diamond. Alison and Michael take on a pitfall palace in Guildford, while Jules and Al tackle a creaky Cornwall seven-bed BandB.

programme.title: George Clarkes Building Home
programme.desc: Kazal and Eleena are ditching Dubai and moving back home to Solihull to be close to extended family. Can they overcome building blunders to create the ultimate hosting hub?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start. But Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?

programme.title: Lake District Rescue
programme.desc: Keswick Mountain Rescue team are called to help a fallen scrambler with head and neck injuries whos been precariously lodged at the edge of a waterfall for hours. And a marathon brings its own challenges as temperatures soar towards 30 degrees.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A pedestrian is rushed to AandE with multiple life-threatening injuries after being injured in a hit-and-run. An acrobat has a damaged wrist. And a 93-year-old has a broken leg.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A pedestrian is rushed to AandE with multiple life-threatening injuries after being injured in a hit-and-run. An acrobat has a damaged wrist. And a 93-year-old has a broken leg.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Jessica Fostekew and Joe enjoy a Canarian caper in Lanzarote, as they trike through the lunar landscape, sample volcanic vino and cactus croquettes, and explore the Jameos del Agua lava tunnels.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: The Beast from the East descends, covering parts of the UK in a blanket of snow and ice and bringing some of the countrys busiest roads to a standstill. Phones are ringing off the hook at as scores of drivers are left stranded on Britains roads.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A dangerous drug dealer lets far more than the cat out of the bag, name swapping friends leave the interceptors baffled, and Phil has a shock for an illegal driver while Mac deals with a drunk drummer.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Mac stops a driver for having no insurance and unearths a huge drugs haul and the chopper tracks down two notorious burglars. Elsewhere, Tim and Ange stop an alleged road-rage suspect armed with a table leg, and Phil wakes up the neighbours in his search for a couple of taxi-fare dodgers.

programme.title: Cahill US Marshal
programme.desc: A lawman is caught in a quandary when his own young sons stand poised on the brink of a life of crime.

programme.title: Gunfight at the O.K. Corral
programme.desc: The friendship between deadly gunfighter Doc Holliday and respected lawman Marshal Wyatt Earp forms the basis of this western saga. It builds up to the showdown at Tombstone, when the Holliday-Earp partnership destroyed the hated Clanton Gang.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Craig Lebeau and his team are pushed to the limit when their main driver gets stuck in a serious accident with a fully loaded truck. They wonder if the money is worth the risks.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A Ford Fiesta stolen by teenagers leads the cops on a chase through Bradford. A gang of burglars who have been targeting pensioners are spotted in Halifax. The road crime team are tracking a team of burglars who operate in a convoy of cars.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: An alleged burglar claims his sack full of money is a gift from his grandma; the discovery of a pink bandana leads deputies to question a suspects story; a deputy attempts to get a man to cool off after a fight with his wife.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A deputy questions a driver about why his broken-down car looks like its been in an accident; obstacle after obstacle prevents a woman from fixing her janky vehicle; a young man looking for a fresh start falls on hard times.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder is being stalked by someone from his past. John Barnett, a criminal whom he played a large part in capturing, has returned to get revenge.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Americas Speeders
programme.desc: In the middle of New Jerseys car theft epidemic, Patrolmen Maurice Marsilla and Bill Gibson pull over a suspicious Lamborghini that is 1000 miles from home; they discover that the driver is a wanted man but he doesnt want to stick around.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Matt Adams is rustling up his roughest ride ever: Macca McReady sails into trouble with a precious load, and the Stephens brothers battle backyards an bogs to rescue their haul.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Gary, Simon and Mouse recover a lorry thats been blown off the road and tipped onto its side. Sean tackles a 12-tonne transporter loaded with a broken-down BMW thats stuck in a ditch. (S9 Ep4)

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Pat faces a tough challenge after a fully loaded 40-tonne lorry crashes, ploughing into a hedge and landing on its side. Michaels called to recover a tanker full of animal waste. (S9 Ep5)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A pursuit through Sheffield lands two known offenders in deep trouble, Phil pulls over a Lamborghini that is not all that it seems to be, and Mac has a close shave when he comes face to face with a gang of hoodies. Elsewhere, Ange gets the runaround from a four-legged foe.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Tim is almost mown down by a desperate driver, Adams patience is tested to the limit by a drunken road-hog, and Ange gets spooked while on the lookout for a prowler, and a group of youths gets one over on the interceptors.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: At Christmas the traffic cops have their hands full as party-going drunks end up getting behind the wheel. The stresses of surviving Yuletide lead to late night argy-bargy and screaming matches.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Police chase a stolen Renault until the driver crashes; a strip search reveals Class A drugs; police bust a large-scale cannabis farm; a man forgets to pay his gas bill.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Police interceptors pursue a drunk driver whos suspected to be armed with a knife. A cross-county biker gang pays Nottingham a visit. Around 27 motorbikes and quads cause absolute mayhem on the street.

programme.title: Tour Championship Snooker
programme.desc: In the final elite tournament before the World Championships, snookers top 12 players compete for the Tour Championship title, live from Manchester Central. Katie Shanahan presents.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: Two men acting strangely in a gas station draw the suspicion of an employee; deputies arrest a man for creating an unsafe environment for his younger brother; a mum calls the cops when her daughter throws water on her.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: The rough terrain of a high-speed pursuit proves both dangerous and useful for deputies; police find more than a stolen air conditioner in the trunk of a messy van; a confused man loses track of whats in his pockets.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully receive information from Deep Throat about a UFO that was shot down over Iraq and has been secretly transported to the US.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Americas Speeders
programme.desc: Patrol Officer Malek Badeer stops a car with a dangerous DIY repair job; patrolman Matt Rugels routine traffic stop proves unfortunate for a wanted man.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Tanker driver Sludge must try and dodge disaster, Steve Graham suffers from his worst nightmare on the road and Macca McReady hits rough weather which throws him into jeopardy.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Sean and Daniel attempt a risky recovery after a lorry loaded with rotten potatoes has rolled, blocking a busy London route. In Scotland, a van has plunged into Loch Eck. (S9 Ep6)

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: On a busy A-road, the Alpha team rescues a lorry stuck in deep mud after it veers off-road, while on the M25, Lantern Recovery responds when a slide-bed loses its wheels. (S9 Ep7)

programme.desc: A global internet outage causes systems to go down so the team are back to basics. In paediatrics, Henry, has fallen off a climbing and shattered his front teeth.

programme.desc: Novice caravan salesman Ant starts a new life on the Costa del Sol, Kiki battles the blues, Kaye opens a pet superstore, and Beckii and Simon face a new family challenge.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Jojo and Bambi take a big risk for their caravan venture, Mark and Lisa squeeze in some Benidorm fun, and tribute singer Ben swaps Gary Barlow for Neil Diamond.

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: It is an all-out turf war between St Bernards Brodie and Rosie and little cockapoo Jasper. The relentless threat of conflict is stressing everyone out. Graeme revisits Labradoodle Doddle. He also catches up with Ray, Tammy, and Welsh Terrier Henry.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North of York, Julian is called to the site of a fatal car crash. The cops also disturb two break-in suspects, leading to a 100mph chase across a golf course.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An elderly lady has collapsed in her bathroom, jamming the door shut. An elderly man driving without his glasses is responsible for a hit and run. And, a people carrier has collided with a mobility scooter.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A serious five-car crash requires an airlift to hospital for the worst-hit driver. An injured man lies in the road, but with no signs of a crash or other vehicles. An un-MOTd car flees cops at 100mph with a nine-year-old in the back.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Damo Stevens takes on an aggressive arsonist intent on bringing Darlington to a standstill, dog handler Andy Hunt is called to a violent domestic disturbance, and Sergeant Kev Salter is hot on the heels of a runaway quad bike.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Kev and Steve go on the hunt for suspected heroin dealers and reveals a shocking way to rewire your house, while dog handler Liam attends the scene of a suspected arson attack. Meanwhile Damo deals with a dozy drunk driver, and another man is so sizzled behind the steering wheel that he blows three-and-a-half times over the limit.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Justin and Paul chase down a gang of suspected car thieves. It takes seven interceptors to stop a violent thief who tries to sink his teeth into them. A drug raid turns life-threatening, and dog-handler Jimmy tries to make sense of a bloke with a blooded face.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britains roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Cash reveals to Harper that David has taken him off Tanes case because of his friendship with Tane, but also the fact Cash was the one who posted Tanes bail.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Although Remi is battling the side effects of his treatment, Eden enlists he and Cash to help plan a treat for Sonny.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: When a patient suffering from chronic vertigo enters his surgery, Dr Laird leaps into action. Dr Shah uses a tuning fork to assess a mans tinnitus.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Nurse Chloe treats 88-year-old Enid, who has been knocked over by high-speed winds and has a severe open head wound. Meanwhile, doctor Mason and doctor Staunton meet 44-year-old Darren who has been rushed in suffering from severe chest pains.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Following specialist emergency response teams on their most extreme medical call outs, embedded with the Critical Care and Air Ambulance Response cars.

programme.title: AandE After Dark
programme.desc: Lifting the lid on the night shift as experienced by AandE workers, with exclusive access to Hull Royal Infirmary. The night-time challenges facing NHS staff are captured in their raw form.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: The teams new dermatologist Dr Magnus Lynch specialises in facial surgery and makes an explosive start with a large jawline cyst. Plus a man with infectious warts.

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: In Suffolk, a series of farm thefts wreak havoc until CCTV provides the vital clues police need to crack the case. Plus shocking footage of the killing of protected wildlife.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Two very different patients come in to consult the doctors about their pregnancy - a young couple wanting confirmation that they really are expecting their first baby, and a woman with a history of postnatal depression worried about slipping back into a depressive state now that she is expecting her fourth child.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Four-year-old Skyler is rushed into Paediatrics care with recurring seizures; 65-year-old Shaun has tumbled down some steps; and Eric, 85, has a badly swollen leg.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Twenty-six-year-old Curtis has a condition thats been troubling him since he was ten. Plus, an operation to remove a facial cyst thats lying close to the temporal nerve.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs. Isolated for millions of years, the weird and wonderful animals marooned here are like nowhere else on Earth. In the north of this island continent is the Daintree, one of the worlds oldest tropical forests.

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the sumptuous treasures of the Golden Age of Egyptian art, starting with troubling psychological portraits of tyrant king Senwosret III, and ending with the golden mask of boy-king Tutankhamun.

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson presents the panel game of word definitions and deceptions. Team captains Patrick Campbell and Frank Muir are joined by guests Lesley-Anne Down, Anthony Valentine, Jilly Cooper and Quentin Bell.

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Chairman Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Paul Jennings and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore.

programme.title: Pompeii: The Mystery of the People Frozen in Time
programme.desc: The city of Pompeii uniquely captures the publics imagination; in 79AD a legendary volcanic disaster left its citizens preserved in ashes to this very day. Yet no-one has been able to unravel the full story that is at the heart of our fascination: how did those bodies become frozen in time? For the first time the BBC has been granted unique access to these strange, ghost-like body casts.

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Brenda Emmanus meets abstract artist Frank Bowling, who became the first black Royal Academician in 2005, to coincide with a major retrospective of his work at Tate Britain.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs. Isolated for millions of years, the weird and wonderful animals marooned here are like nowhere else on Earth. In the north of this island continent is the Daintree, one of the worlds oldest tropical forests.

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness is the ability to be present with a clear, calm, curious mind. And feelings of joy can be triggered when this happens. How can watching penguins pinching pebbles, seeing antelope leaping in the air or looking at scenes of summer flowers help us to feel more positive emotionally? Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe takes us on a global journey with imagery that will bring feelings of happiness and wellbeing to the viewer.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Inferno in the Fields and the Fight to Save Badgers
programme.desc: Firefighters and farmers tackle Bedfordshire field fires, rescue crews respond to a horse in danger, and Paul Colgan helps volunteers protect wildlife from illegal badger baiters.

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: The search for a vulnerable young woman unravels a tragic case of neglect and murder. And police investigating the murder of a popular teen find the key suspects very close to home.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martin and Scarlett Moffatt
programme.desc: Turn the Gogglebox on and tune in to see Martin and special celebrity guest presenter Scarlett Moffatt take a tour of a Sunderland property that has them looking to break out the champagne. We also delve into the archives from 2007 to see a London studio flat thats at a price Martin just cant believe. And X marks the spot for some dodgy patio doors for Jacqui in Surrey.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp has a puzzle for passers-by in Brackley to solve. Her mystery object is heavy and made of cast iron with a brass opening. What could it be and why is it so important for one avid collector. The venue for the shopping showdown is an antiques centre in the Northamptonshire town. Engaged couple Aron and Charlie are bargain-hunting with Thomas Forrester, while married couple Lucille and Geoff are with Tim Weeks.

programme.desc: A former pig farmer makes a last-ditch attempt to clear her home of unwanted belongings and antiques before old age prevents her decade-long fundraising campaign for an African charity.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in Sussex to help a couple from a Berkshire commuter town escape to the country. They have requested a unique home, and the quirkier, the better! The selection on offer includes some truly wow-factor properties, any one of which could be the mystery house. Denise also visits the Royal Botanic Gardens at Wakehurst to discover how climate change could shape the way we all garden in the future.

programme.title: Farm 999

programme.desc: Firefighters and farmers tackle Bedfordshire field fires, rescue crews respond to a horse in danger, and Paul Colgan helps volunteers protect wildlife from illegal badger baiters.

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: The search for a vulnerable young woman unravels a tragic case of neglect and murder. And police investigating the murder of a popular teen find the key suspects very close to home.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martin and Scarlett Moffatt
programme.desc: Turn the Gogglebox on and tune in to see Martin and special celebrity guest presenter Scarlett Moffatt take a tour of a Sunderland property that has them looking to break out the champagne. We also delve into the archives from 2007 to see a London studio flat thats at a price Martin just cant believe. And X marks the spot for some dodgy patio doors for Jacqui in Surrey.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp has a puzzle for passers-by in Brackley to solve. Her mystery object is heavy and made of cast iron with a brass opening. What could it be and why is it so important for one avid collector. The venue for the shopping showdown is an antiques centre in the Northamptonshire town. Engaged couple Aron and Charlie are bargain-hunting with Thomas Forrester, while married couple Lucille and Geoff are with Tim Weeks.

programme.desc: A round-up of the days news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads into crocodile infested waters for a spooky nocturnal encounter with a truly shocking predator, the electric eel. Later on, he takes a boat ride up the Amazon river to meet another infamous water dwelling killer - the black piranha; the largest of all piranha species. He also braves a thunderstorm to get a close-up view of one of the worlds rarest canines, the maned wolf.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve enlists the help of an elephant to track down a very grumpy rhinoceros. Later, he stalks the forests in search of the largest cat on earth, the highly elusive and endangered Bengal Tiger. He also has a chance encounter with a hive of honeybees in the centre of a busy Nepalese town before taking to the skies to paraglide with an Egyptian vulture above the foothills of the Himalayas.

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: The school holds an open day, but things go badly wrong when Kim is assaulted by a pupil. Meanwhile, Tom and Izzies affair continues.

programme.title: Murdered by My Boyfriend
programme.desc: Tells the true story of what happens to a teenage girl when she falls in love with the wrong man. The everyday story of young love turns dark and sinister when the handsome and charming stranger seeks to dominate every aspect of the womans life.

programme.title: Murdered by My Father
programme.desc: Every parent wants the best for their kids, and Shahzad is no exception. Ever since his wife died hes been trying to keep his two kids Salma and Hassan on track. Salma is growing up quickly, and Shahzad wants to make sure shes set up with the right guy to settle down with. Its a promise he made his wife, and part of what he considers his duty as a dad.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Evie throws herself headlong at life, Suz and Josh worry its too much, too soon. Meanwhile, Noa realises he has to face up to his feelings...

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Her world rocked, Evie has retreated to the safety of the garage. But with a new understanding, Suz and Izzie create a unique version of Film Club to bring back Evies smile.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Noa didnt want a big goodbye, but now the days finally come for him to leave, will Evie let him go that easily? Its not the only big decision she has to face...

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Irresponsible friends Conor and Jock are up to their old tricks, this time trying to outsmart the police/Gardaí by doing the exact opposite of what they would expect, with disastrous consequences. Sergeant Healy catches the lads wearing their trademark masks in the act and a high speed chase ensues, involving some innocent bystanders along the way.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Gavin, a new pupil at school who has some history with Jock, ignites a jealousy in Conor which brings a tense situation to comical heights.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: A day trip in memory of Conors dad and the appearance some mysterious cash cause confusion, with the interference of Billy Murphy creating a perfect storm of mayhem.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor, Jock and Mairead, always tight on cash, come up with a hare-brained idea to become business owners as they buy a second hand chip van to try make extra money.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Billy Murphy enlists the help of Conor, Jock, and eventually Mairead, to avoid getting killed by a former cellmate of his who has just been released from prison.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The baby is due and everyone is frustrated with the nonstop arguing of both families, so an ill-advised weekend break for all is organised.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Sharks are a mainstay of Steves Deadly 60, slicing through his ratings with their size, speed and power. They are among the most spectacular predators on the planet - but theyre in trouble.

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.sub-title: International Friendly: Wales v Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Huw Morgan presents coverage as Wales face Northern Ireland in an international friendly match at the Cardiff City Stadium.

programme.title: Muslim Matchmaker
programme.desc: Fulani looks for connection, Mariam gets serious, and Yasmin and Hoda worry about Noureen.

programme.title: Muslim Matchmaker
programme.desc: Noureen faces an unexpected challenge with her match while Hoda meets with new client Uneeb.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: After a three-minute video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: As Mobeens investigation deepens, it becomes clearer that illicit relationships between prisoners and jail staff are just the tip of the iceberg. The bigger story involves organised criminal gangs determined to cash in on a lucrative prison drug economy. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are set the task of creating a TV advert for Volkswagen. Despite vowing to work together to deliver a classic piece of advertising, the boys quickly fall out on set. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond looks for an alternative to boring Germanic sports saloons and ends up going mostly sideways in the new Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst. Plus, American chat show legend Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Long Story Short
programme.desc: Comedy short.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Evie throws herself headlong at life, Suz and Josh worry its too much, too soon. Meanwhile, Noa realises he has to face up to his feelings...

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Her world rocked, Evie has retreated to the safety of the garage. But with a new understanding, Suz and Izzie create a unique version of Film Club to bring back Evies smile.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Noa didnt want a big goodbye, but now the days finally come for him to leave, will Evie let him go that easily? Its not the only big decision she has to face...

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: The team restores a bowling club sign in Scotlands smallest county and a Victorian clock with haunted Hebridean roots. Meanwhile, Will learns coppersmithing for whisky stills.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Its sisters versus friends in this episode from Somerset. In the saleroom, both experts surprise their teams with silver - but which bonus buys will the bidders take a shine to?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Tim tackles a battered unicycle, Mark fixes an iconic 80s gadget, Brenton takes on an Edwardian plate camera, and David repairs a model of a virus that changed medical history.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the days game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: As long as the waters warm, Clive loves to swim, so hes enthusiastic about taking a dip in the vast volcanic spring of Saturnia and discovering why Italians believe in the healing power of thermal water. Travelling on through Maremma - a big stretch of country running between Tuscany and Lazio - Clive stays in a treehouse at a lavender farm and learns how to extract oil from the plants.

programme.title: Big Little Journeys
programme.desc: In the forests of western Madagascar, a tiny chameleon emerges from the nest. It is the beginning of one of the strangest of all journeys. Her urge is to climb and reach the high branches where she can hide amongst the leaves.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: In Lochgilphead, in Argyll and Bute, Michael examines a valuable painting on an asbestos tile, a pair of Staffordshire zebras, a painting by Scottish artist Hamilton McKenzie, and a carved-bone ship made by Napoleonic prisoners of war.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! has come to Blackburn, where the valuation day is being held at Blackburn Cathedral. Anita Manning discovers an Omar Ramsden spoon, and Charles Hanson finds an old silver bucket that has been used as a bin and a Christmas tree holder.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Each week a group of four celebrities compete to test their knowledge in a series of quick-paced rounds of games. Host, Richard Osman then cross-examines the celebrities as they strive to increase their scores and match Richards mental prowess.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Rick rediscovers his love for the foodie heaven of Argyll, where land and loch yield exquisite produce. He meets chef Pamela Brunton, a Michelin Green Star winner, whose menu showcases local and foraged ingredients.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Services critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: I Tackled a Terrorist with a Narwhal Tusk
programme.desc: Honouring the man who used a narwhal tusk to tackle the London Bridge terrorist. Can sculptor Nick capture the essence of the hero?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions in another semi-final. Specialist subjects include George Frideric Handel, the films of Christopher Nolan and the US Masters tournament from 1990 to the present.

programme.desc: Alice is in Lincoln, getting to grips with life in medieval England. She visits the magnificent cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-lives the battle at Lincoln Castle.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in London, as she attempts to make sense of the world-changing events during the short but eventful Restoration period. She learns the violent and vengeful truth behind Charles IIs carefully crafted public image.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers are in Bradford where Jo Brayshaw is in the dark over some Russian binoculars and Tim Hogarth is brimming with confidence over a Lalique bowl, but will David Hakeney let a vintage train set go to auction for the sake of a tenner?

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick and Corey discover a newfound love for an old Buick Roadmaster; Chum checks out a doll and volleyball signed by Tom Hanks; a physical bitcoin comes into the shop; a samurai sword lands on the counter.

programme.desc: Bing and Sula are planting seeds at Ammas when Coco arrives with her new skipping rope. Coco teaches them how to skip, with Sula picking it up quicker than Bing. programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas go ice skating with some furry friends during winter.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: As Honey waits impatiently for her appointment with Dr Bingo, the family distracts her by pretending to work in the hospital.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee hosts a Buddy Bonanza for Mrs Weaver and the Squirrels, with the aim of finding new buddies with common interests to them.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Incy Wincy Spider
programme.desc: Hedgley sings a song about Incy Wincy Spider.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.sub-title: Golden Chicken and Lamp Post Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to get to the allotment to pick some vegetables. On the way they cheer up a sad lamp post, count loaves of bread in the bakers window, jump in muddy puddles and even find a golden chicken!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ted and his family visit a wildlife park and Ted gets to be an animal keeper for the day. First, he feeds the hungry deer. Theyre very friendly. Next, its onto the bison, who show Ted their giant tongues.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Connor lives in Scotland. He makes a delicious vegetarian haggis, to share with his friends. He serves his tasty haggis with freshly mashed neeps and tatties. As an extra special surprise, Connor has arranged for a special guest to play the Scottish bagpipes as the haggis is served, and his friends have no idea about this special surprise! They are all looking forward to trying the food, but they were not very keen on fresh coriander, which is one of the ingredients.

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Emily wants to find out more about puffins in this episode, so Rory and Sandy head to a very special Scottish island to search for them. Theyre able to see where they nest in burrows and hunt for food by diving into the sea. And Chantelle joins Aneeshwar, who is a keen bird watcher - together they try to spot a puffling, a baby puffin, in the cliffs. Chantelle and Rorys fact files help answer all of Emilys questions.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Newfoundland Sylvie is a big therapy dog with big soft cuddles who loves to help people when theyre worried. She and her owner, Katrina, visit all sorts of people every day, young and old. Today Sylvie and Katrina are visiting a doctors surgery to help a little boy called Ben. Ben has fallen off his scooter and grazed his knee, and his mum says hes too nervous to come into the doctors room. Sylvies mission is to help Ben feel brave enough to see Dr Stevens.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Snow Leopard and Yak
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a snow leopard and some yaks.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Doug and Waffle share fun memories as they make their new neighbour, Pepper, a dog-bed.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona learns to love her colourful beak.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this swinging homage to the Sinatra era, the Small Potatoes sing about how theyre no average spuds.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: A samba band is coming to play, and everyone is helping to prepare. Stripes is so busy that hes forgotten one thing - to book the band! With no band coming, all seems lost, until Yukee and Stripes realise the Sunhouse already has its own brilliant band!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is working on an inventory of the clubhouses supplies to plan for the future. The Squirrels wonder what the future is like - will Enid still be a cat? Could the clubhouse be in space? Luckily, Duggee has his Future Badge and many theories about the answers to their questions. The Squirrels are transported to a very different-looking future clubhouse, with a very different-sounding robot Duggee.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `bee and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different bees and find out all about them.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Nine and friends learn all about capacity.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock I is having a dance party - by herself! Can her friends help her see the light on disco night?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends meet some very cool, feathered friends and learn to fly them. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is taking a trip on an aeroplane... but who is the pilot?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It is night time and the Teletubbies are all sleeping, apart from Tinky Winky. Stepping outside, Tinky Winky sees a star in the sky which sings Twinkle Twinkle.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Rabbit has a problem: hes invited everyone to his big circus, but they dont think its big at all! Can Patchwork Rabbit save the show?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Raa Raa has got a new pogo stick. He is having a great time bouncing around, but his friends arent so sure he seems to be causing rather a lot of damage. They try to find a way Raa Raa can practise without destroying their jungle home.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Being a super chugger is hard work, but when given a well deserved day off, Action Chugger has a hard time relaxing.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Rapids Johnson enjoys an ice cream on his search for the Ice Cream Bear and Storybook Stan is distracted by some chewy toffees. When an ice cream seller turns up, Ann Teak finds another use for an antique Viking helmet.

programme.title: Andys Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy leaps into his time-travelling clock to visit the fearsome sabre-toothed cat, but when his gizmo malfunctions, he is shrunk down to the size of an insect and must escape from a marauding prehistoric dung beetle!

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Cosmo-players arrive on an underwater planet and start hunting for clues, until the are interrupted by a turtle that cannot find its way home.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When an underwater tremor separates the Gup-X and its crew, Barnacles seeks guidance from a mysterious sea turtle.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Stolen Firewood
programme.desc: When Peter, Lily and Benjamin go out to collect firewood, they soon find that not one but three predators are after some too. Can the rabbits stage a series of daring raids to retrieve it?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tino the artistic mouse is trying to paint a picture of Duggee but cannot get it right, which infuriates him no end. As he tells the Squirrels: seeking perfection is his whole philosophy! The squirrels dont know what that is, so Duggee explains: a philosophy is a set of rules that you live by. But the rules exist in your head - its about how you think, not just what you think about.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Kipp doesnt know what made him giddy, its up to Vida to solve the dizzy mystery.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea tricks Supertato and Broccoli with fake fortune cookies that tell them to go outside the supermarket. Thankfully, Carrot doesnt believe them and saves the day.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy and Spicy have to cover their ears when Sunny learns to play the guitar.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Bryan the ferry cant shut his engine off, so hes speeding out of control around the lake. How will Mojo and Bo get aboard to take control and steer Bryan to safety?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Luckys Dad, Lucky and Chucky have come over to the Heelers house to watch a rugby final together.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piri gang enter a treasure hunt competition but discover they must pay Captain Krill with sprats for clues. Pancake is suspicious. Is there some fishy business going on?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo pretend that they are living on a desert island in the back garden and they discover Dad washed up on the beach. Dad needs to embrace his inner kid if he wants to make Bingo and Blueys Rug Island his home, too.

programme.title: Andys Dino Island
programme.desc: Jen is tracking all the dinosaurs on Dino Island to find out when they move from one part of the island to another in search of food or shelter. She asks Andy to find a herd of Triceratops and place one of her tracking devices on them. Heading out on an epic adventure, Andy finds a lost baby Triceratops, who has become separated from its family because of a thorn in its foot. Andy sets about removing the painful thorn, then reunites the baby with its family.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle tries to impress on his first day as a listening dog at school, and to Mrs Hobbs horror, George the cat goes missing.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafis mermaid wish helps Jake find his courage, and they have a fun underwater tea party.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.desc: When Gran Gran and JoJo find a family recipe for Great Gran Grans signature Saint Lucian banana bread, theyre excited to bake a batch of it. But theres one problem, cake mix has been spilt on the recipe.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Sarahs hair keeps getting in her way, so its time for a trip to the hairdresser.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Three pale things - a shell, a spiral and a cone - drift towards the planet seeking music, heat and light to revive them. Tiny and Small have seen them before and know just what to do. Will they be able to revive the new pale star-shaped thing?

programme.desc: Frasier hires a temporary replacement for a vacationing Roz, but the woman soon begins to take over his radio show.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Short tempers, simmering tensions and downright disrespect are on the menu at Chriss troubled home-cooking cafe in Georgetown, central Texas.

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4s midday news summary.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon and Henry head to north Wales where Henry finds an antique cannon in antique dealers Sue and Joshs storage shed. Simon finds a croquet set in need of restoration.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Matt Baker and his family return, as they make more vital changes to their farm, and at the top of their to-do list is tackling the destruction from the storms.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jill and Ronnie are finally taking the plunge and moving to France. Jasmine Harman lines up five superb options in Languedoc-Roussillon for their £250k budget, but are any perfect for them?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Portugal, friends Richard and Simon raise funds to renovate an abandoned bar. In France, couple Mark and Gary turn an old distillery into a gîte.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Emma Louise and Liv host at their 527-year-old pub in Kent, where spooky, spectral tales and a chaotic bout of drag bingo and karaoke await the BandBers.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Sheffield, first host Lizzy brings the heat with a Mexican-themed night filled with fiesta vibes, a build-your-own burrito bonanza and an unexpected surprise...

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: No stranger to helping others find their perfect place in the sun, Jasmine Harman takes the plunge and transforms a run-down villa into a family home on the Costa del Sol.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: In Nottingham, Emma and Matts hectic schedules mixed with two energetic kids is a recipe for mealtime mayhem. Can they turn chaotic convenience into bountiful batching?

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifteen-year-old Meldred is rushed into AandE after being stabbed in the thigh by a stranger while walking to school. Sports commentator Andrew has an inflamed ankle with blood spots and a rash.

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: The gloves are off as Predator turns on Predator, and a fiery clash sets rivalries alight.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aleshe make decorated traybakes, tarte tatin and recreate their childhood toys in biscuits.

programme.title: Sphinx
programme.desc: An abducted teenager re-appears after three years, but the huge back tattoo isnt the only thing about her thats unrecognisable.

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: For the first time since Edwin, Emmas tempted by romance again, with the handsome David. A sober Adele returns to Fairley Hall, threatening everything for Adam and Olivia. In the 1970s, Emma commands Paula to break off her romance with Jim Fairley.

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Emma prepares the perfect Christmas for her family, but her unrelenting obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. In the 1970s, as Emma recovers from a heart attack, an oblivious Paula doubles down on her love affair with Jim.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George helps a couple whose Weymouth home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood, and revisits a couple in Chiswick to find out whats next on their demolition list.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: While Rachel tries to sort out her mixed feelings for her handsome colleague Gavin, Ross recruits Chandler to help him meet attractive women and make Rachel jealous. Meanwhile, Phoebe regrets bringing Monica to a karaoke night when she takes the microphone and delights in the applause, unaware that the men in the audience are cheering because they can see through her dress, and Joey submits to painful eyebrow waxing.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Blind Dates
programme.desc: Joey and Phoebe conspire to arrange awful dates for both Rachel and Ross, hoping that appreciation of their former relationship will push them back together. Meanwhile, the ovulating Monica wants to get intimate with Chandler.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey stumbles upon a way to impress a famous actor during an audition, Ross learns the identity of a long-ago mugger, and Chandler accepts an internship at a shoe company.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Boob Job
programme.desc: Monica secretly asks Joey for a loan because she and Chandler are strapped for cash, Phoebe invites her boyfriend to move in with her, and Rachels apartment baby-proofing drives Joey crazy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Memorial Service
programme.desc: When Chandler jokingly posts an outrageous biography about Ross on Ross college alumni website, Ross retaliates in kind, sparking an internet war. Meanwhile, Monica gets extremely vigilant after a weak-willed Phoebe asks for help to stop her from contacting Mike after their relationship ends.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Hoping to win a huge jackpot, the friends pool their money and buy dozens of lottery tickets, but they argue over how to spend the fortune if they win, and Monica irritates everyone else by buying tickets just for her and Chandler. Exasperated, Phoebe takes the bowl of everyones tickets and threatens to throw it off the balcony. The others make up, but a pigeon startles Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A visit to the set of a soap opera inspires Rachel to dream of a romantic encounter with Joey, and Chandler must take Ross on a weekend getaway after Monica is unable to take time off from work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Soap Opera Party
programme.desc: Ross is excited when he meets a stunning palaeontology professor, but when he takes her to Joeys rooftop party for the actors soap-opera co-stars, she intimidates him with her impressive list of former boyfriends. Meanwhile, Rachel and an intoxicated Monica make fools of themselves at the party chasing after the hunky daytime actors.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fertility Test
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler have an embarrassing encounter with an old acquaintance at a fertility clinic, Ross plays Cyrano to help Joey woo a professor, and Rachel sneaks into a spa for a massage hoping Phoebe wont find out.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Disappointed to learn that they cannot conceive a baby naturally, Monica and Chandler consider their options. When Chandler invites a handsome colleague round for dinner, Monica and Chandler embarrassingly interview him as a potential sperm donor. Meanwhile, while shopping with Phoebe, Rachel admits that she still has a thing for Joey.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys answer an urgent call from Central AK, where they find a family with all the infrastructure but minimal skills to realise their dream. With subzero temps looming, the Raneys must help them winter-proof and set them up for the spring ahead.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: With a family facing ruin, setbacks, and soaring debt on their BC mine, its up to Juan and prospecting experts Jeff and Sophia Bond to save them. Using 6,000 pounds of steel, Juan creates his largest-ever hopper build to get a massive gold increase.

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: The team uncovers a potential Bigfoot kill zone and launch a squadron of patrol drones to sweep the area for the existence of Sasquatch.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: As the winter season arrives, the fleet braces for a global weather pattern known as Super El Niño, an atmospheric phenomenon forecast to unleash a gauntlet of cyclonic storms across the Bering Sea. Captains and crews prepare for the worst.

Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: With a family facing ruin, setbacks, and soaring debt on their BC mine, its up to Juan and prospecting experts Jeff and Sophia Bond to save them. Using 6,000 pounds of steel, Juan creates his largest-ever hopper build to get a massive gold increase.

programme.desc: Thriller set against the background of the gold rush in the Canadian Rockies. A man jailed for murder falls for the beautiful saloon singer who looked after his son while he was inside. He tries to conceal his criminal past from his son, but that is the least of their problems when the trio are left defenceless against rampaging Indians.

programme.desc: A lighthearted take on family life in which a St Louis banker takes his wife and numerous children for a troubled holiday in an ocean-front home that leaves him wishing he was back at work.

programme.desc: Violent thriller about a Vietnam veteran who turns to crime after falling on hard times. Depressed and out of work upon returning home from the war to a neighbourhood in steep decline, he decides to join a gang of criminals planning a high-risk robbery.

programme.desc: Chicken pot pie; steamed mussels; jambalaya; chargrilled oysters; hot chicken; homemade soups and salads.

programme.desc: In Denver, homemade Tater Tots, pork rinds and a pastrami sandwich, in Tacoma, pork shoulder tacos, elk sliders and Tater Tots stuffed with parmesan cheese.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richmans quest for good eats lands him in Tampa, Florida - land of sunny skies and authentic flavours. Adam stops by Skippers Smokehouse to try a Floridian speciality - alligator ribs.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to New Orleans to taste the best of the Big Easy, including a mammoth po boy and an ice cream sundae packed with eight large scoops of ice cream, smothered with eight different toppings.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Ships Ahoy, Marys Mix-Up and Elevator Error
programme.desc: Its Fleet Week in New York and Navy ships are in port. The bakery creates an amazing cake to honour the Navys service men and women.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Fathers Day Collapse, Buddys Surprise, and Tee Time
programme.desc: The crew makes a basketball-inspired cake as a Fathers Day surprise for a hoop-mad dad, but disaster strikes during delivery!

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: Rick samples whelks in Bognor Regis, prepares steak and kidney pudding, explores the herbal delights of a walled garden in Sussex, and backs a campaign to save the sea bass.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: A visit to East Anglia and the Midlands, where he comes across a host of specialities, including Norfolk Black turkeys, Lincolnshire chine, speciality pork pies and the champion of British cheeses, Stilton.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers explore a region close to Sis heart: Tyne and Wear and its great city of Newcastle, where he spent most of his life. After weeks travelling across the stunning northern countryside, the bikers are having an urban adventure.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her secrets of home cooking and celebrating.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree plays around with fuss-free food with no cooking required; she makes a ranch muffuletta sandwich; a rotisserie chicken is the secret to her Chinese chicken salad with ginger and chili dressing; she finishes with magical Mermaid Ice Cream.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes sticky toffee pudding with his mum Sally. He also rustles up the perfect steak and onion sandwich, and cooks an Italian classic - osso buco with saffron risotto.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie takes on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat: chicken Kiev. He also cooks spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist, and creates a deluxe shepherds pie - made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder instead of mince.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas built a fabulous library at the barn and now its time to say thanks to the team that built it. Ina prepares fig and fennel caponata, an antipasto platter, and Tuscan mashed chickpeas.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its Inas idea of a perfect day - while Jeffrey runs on the beach, she whips up raspberry muffins and coffee for their breakfast. Then she heads to local restaurant, South Fork Kitchen.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Once again 12 new bakers don their aprons and head for the iconic tent in the heart of the British Countryside. The bakers first signature challenge is to make a Madeira cake and their first showstopper is the classic Black Forest gateau.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Theres 11 bakers left in the tent and they face the signature challenge of creating 24 biscotti. The technical challenge is arlettes and the showstopper is an edible biscuit box of 36 biscuits of a different flavour.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands. He is joined by top Scottish Michelin starred chef Tom Kitchin. Their journey begins with an incredible tour of the castle grounds complete with awe inspiring views. James kicks off the cooking with his venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Next Tom Kitchin dusts of his wellies and heads in to the woods for a bit of wild mushroom foraging.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Tom are making their way to Edinburgh but before they can do that, James makes a detour to Edradour whisky distillery in Pitlochry where he makes a delicious whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once theyve reached Edinburgh, Tom shares his local knowledge by visiting one of his favourite suppliers, Welch Fishmongers. He learns the secret to their amazing smoked salmon which James uses for a salmon mousse with cucumber and dill pickle and melba toast.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers explore a region close to Sis heart: Tyne and Wear and its great city of Newcastle, where he spent most of his life. After weeks travelling across the stunning northern countryside, the bikers are having an urban adventure.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks up a hearty chicken and vegetable pie, super-satisfying mushroom risotto, beautiful homemade bread and a no-cook chocolatey fridge cake, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a hearty fish pie, helped by his son Buddy, with a delicate fish curry made with coconut milk and spinach for the sauce.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: In the final programme of the series, Jamie Oliver cooks his versions of three comfort food classics. Theres ham, egg and chips, with a Jamaican jerk rub for the ham and served with caramelized pineapple. His ultimate chicken kebab is served with hummus and mango amba sauce.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Burbank, Calif., a chili-serving spot with a name dating back a century, Philadelphia, a three-story gourmet bar food joint, Toronto, Canada, a smokehouse kicking burgers and fried chicken up a notch.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Denver, a corner bar smoking pork for its biscuits and topping pizzas, in Scottsdale, Ariz., breakfast enchiladas and french toast with red wine rosemary syrup.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Philadelphia, fried PBandJs and tacos that start with the head of a pig, in Phoenix, pork sammie, and cooking everything in its wood-fired oven.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Phoenix, a regional pork dish, in Toronto, Canada, sticky Asian pork ribs and a Canadian staple.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to Portland, Oregon, to taste mouthwatering Peruvian chicken, a succulent porchetta sandwich and a burrito packed with ten of the spiciest peppers in the world.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to Chicago for some epic Windy City eats, including a massive triple-threat pork sandwich and a challenge featuring a 1kg double bacon cheeseburger and two pounds of Irish Nacho cheese fries.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy and the team make an erupting volcano cake for a science camp. Also theres a movie-themed cake with a projection screen inside!

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The guys reflect on the life of Momma Mary and her legacy. While the family grieves, they also celebrate her triumphant life and vivacious personality.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day, and after the disastrous fish course Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis are all desperate to impress the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis last chance to impress with their desserts as at the end of todays cooking one chef must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort, who must decide which chef will win a place in the finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of London and the South Easts superstar chefs, returning contender Adam Simmonds, Michelin-starred newcomer Tom Sellers and his former boss Adam Byatt, attempt to dazzle the mystery judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: London and South East chefs, Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt continue their battle. Does a trip to Adam Byatts nans house to find out about his grandfathers military connections spur him on to fish course glory?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt go head to head cooking their main courses. At the crucial half way stage the chefs must do all they can to score highly.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert course time as the London and South East chefs compete for one of two places in the judges chamber and the competition couldnt be tighter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a tightly fought battle today as the two remaining London and South East chefs cook all four of their dishes again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge, Joy Hunter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central regions top chefs, returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett go into battle. They begin with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett fight it out for fish course glory. After plating up late last time will Aktar manage to be on time today?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central region chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poynton and Jason Hodnett go head to head cooking their main courses for the veteran judge. With Mark and Aktar fighting for the lead can Jason sneak under the radar to take them by surprise?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Central chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poyton and Jason Hodnett are all striving for one of two spots in the regional final. Will Jasons elaborate chocolate medal dessert see him triumph or sent home empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central region chefs cook all four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge Max Hastings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by Ken Sturdy who landed on Sword Beach on D-Day and who helped judge the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the half-way point in finals week and those chefs who have yet to get a dish onto the shortlist are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by Baroness Trumpington who worked as a cypher clerk at Bletchley Park from 1941 to 1946.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the chefs to get their dish in contention for the final banquet and those whove failed to finish in the top three so far are going all out. Joining the judges is Celia Sandys, Winston Churchills granddaughter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the moment of truth as the victorious four must put their finest hours on a plate for those who served both on the front line and the home front, such as D-Day veterans Ken Sturdy and George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands top chefs, Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee, and Graham Campbell - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge. Having missed out last year, returning finalist Jak is determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell fight it out for the fish course. Graham is in a confident mood but returning finalist Jak and self-taught Jimmy are pushing him hard. Will the pressure prove too much for ambitious Graham?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell are battling to plate up a perfect main course for this weeks veteran chef. Self-taught Jimmy is honouring his mum with his rendition of her Chinese beef stew.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today its the Scottish chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow, and with one chef in the lead after a triumphant main, the pressures on for the other two to plate up perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, who will help decide which chef will go forward to the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the south wests finest chefs - Jude Kereama, Josh Eggleton and Dominic Chapman - attempt to impress a veteran judge with their starters. As the only newcomer in the kitchen, Jude is determined to plate up a winning dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three south west chefs fight it out for the fish course. Despite schoolboy errors during the starter, experienced Dominic is determined to get a high score on the course. But will newcomer Jude, a seafood master, overcome his nerves to plate up?.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course as the three south-west chefs go head to head. Newcomer Jude is brimming with determination and confidence as the main course battle hits the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The south-west chefs have one last chance to impress as they face the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges; with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining south-west judges must cook their entire menus again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Joining the judges is Radhika Bynon, a member of the Forest Gate Womens Institute, a modern urban group of women.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael are challenged to plate up perfect starter courses for this weeks veteran judge. Its the first time in the Great British Menu kitchen for all three chefs, so who will triumph and who will fall?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael fight it out for the fish course. Experimental chef Stephen is determined to prove himself with a fish course honouring the WIs tradition of preserving, but hes up against stiff competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with the Welsh chefs going head to head. With all three chefs paying homage to Welsh lamb; Adam with a refined faggot, Phil with a dish dedicated to his Mum and Stephen with a lamb dish celebrating the WI.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the tricky dessert course and the last chance for the Welsh chefs to secure their place in tomorrows regional final. With everything to play for, the pressure is fierce. Only two can make it through, and one will leave the competition for good.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two remaining Welsh chefs cook their four courses again for the judges and Rosemary Bishton. One chef will be chosen to represent Wales in the National Finals taking them a step closer to cooking at the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Northern Irelands top chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold - go head to head with their starter courses to impress this weeks veteran judge. Former champion Chris is back for glory and determined to reach the banquet, but hes up against two newcomers with their sights firmly set on victory.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold are cooking their fish courses. Ambitious Ben is determined to steal Chriss regional crown, but under fierce scrutiny from this weeks formidable veteran, will the pressure to deliver spell melt down?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of Northern Irelands finest chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold. Ambitious Ben is out to prove himself with a perfect lamb main course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Northern Irish chefs are all striving for one of the two spots in the Regional Final. The pressure is intense, and in the end one chef must leave the competition empty handed.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan is in the heart of the English countryside at Warwick Castle. While it appears idyllic today, for centuries it was a poisoned chalice to the Earls of Warwick who lived there and its walls echo with tales of betrayal, bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the Georgian era in her hometown of Bristol, from architectural gems to the worlds first chocolate factory, backstreet gin and the horror of the slave trade.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Edwardian Britain by visiting Cardiff, which grew rich selling the coal that powered the Empire. Alice hears the stories of the Edwardian miners and their backbreaking and often dangerous work.

programme.title: Truthseekers
programme.desc: Explore the huge Nazca lines that were made over 2,000 years ago and are still a mystery to researchers today.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: After scaling walls and eluding officers to escape from three separate high security prisons in the 50s, Alfie Hinds earned the nickname Houdini.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Focusing on Roman Britannia, where finds include the thickening mystery of 97 baby skeletons found by the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon that turns history on its head, and a Roman cult figure buried for 1700 years beneath a fort.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Viking Age in Britain and visits excavations that are revealing a different side to these seafaring pirates from Scandinavia. She looks for signs of the earliest Viking settlers in the Outer Hebrides, and in Orkney - where Viking dominance outlasted anywhere else in Britain - she visits the excavation of a Viking chiefs citadel and finds evidence of their way-of-life.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan is in the heart of the English countryside at Warwick Castle. While it appears idyllic today, for centuries it was a poisoned chalice to the Earls of Warwick who lived there and its walls echo with tales of betrayal, bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
